name: Bioinformatics Pipeline Class Diagram
input: Create a class diagram for a bioinformatics analysis pipeline. Classes: SequenceData (attributes: fastq_files, quality_scores, read_length; methods: trim_adapters(), filter_quality()), Aligner (attributes: reference_genome, index_path; methods: align(SequenceData), generate_bam()), VariantCaller (attributes: min_quality, min_depth; methods: call_variants(bam_file), filter_variants()), Annotation (attributes: database, version; methods: annotate_variants(vcf_file), predict_impact()), QualityReport (attributes: metrics, pass_threshold; methods: generate_report(), visualize()). Relationships: SequenceData is used by Aligner. Aligner produces output consumed by VariantCaller. VariantCaller output goes to Annotation. QualityReport aggregates metrics from all other classes.
qualityCriteria: All 5 classes present (SequenceData, Aligner, VariantCaller, Annotation, QualityReport)
Attributes listed in each class with types or names
Methods listed in each class with parentheses
Relationships between classes shown with correct direction
No truncated class or method names
Valid Mermaid syntax that parses without errors
napkinBenchmark: Standard UML class diagram with 5 boxes, each containing attributes and methods sections, relationship arrows with labels, clean layout.
